title: HTTP Client Reuse, Retrieval, Redirects, Validators, and Bounded Bodies
description: Each page creates a new client, sends browser-like lies, follows redirects outside scope, reads unlimited bodies, and accepts an HTML-looking error page. Learners cumulatively produce bounded HTTP page-fetch contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Reuse one configured client or bounded pool and close it deliberately so connection, proxy, TLS, cookie, and resource ownership are visible.
Use safe retrieval methods and an honest User-Agent, Accept, Accept-Encoding, conditional, and contact policy without impersonation.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, content coding, and domain outcomes before parsing the representation.
